definition: Fiscal deficits exceeding 4.5% GDP extend correlation regime transitions by a median of approximately 3 years beyond what monetary-only models predict, based on six historical episodes. Current US deficit at ~6.5% GDP exceeds all six precedents.
evidence: Six historical episodes with deficits >4% GDP during transitions
4/6 required exogenous forcing events to resolve
No credible fiscal consolidation path through at least 2028
open_questions: Small sample (n=6) limits confidence in the specific median
Magnitude could be 2-5 years; direction is higher-confidence than magnitude
What constitutes a sufficient exogenous forcing event at current deficit levels?
